Title: Circadian Entrainment in Arabidopsis by the Sugar-Responsive Transcription Factor bZIP63

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: signals cellular sucrose/energy status to entrain circadian phase
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK1 catalytic alpha subunit; sugar/energy-sensing protein kinase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive KIN10 overexpression lengthens circadian period under low-energy conditions and abolishes sucrose-pulse-induced circadian phase advance, indicating KIN10 is required for sugar-responsive circadian entrainment.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression (KIN10-ox); CCA1:luc circadian bioluminescence period assays under low/high light with/without sucrose; phase response curves after sucrose pulses; western blot confirmation of overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KIN10 overexpression further lengthened circadian period specifically under low-energy conditions. 2. Sucrose still shortened period in KIN10-ox during prolonged treatment, suggesting post-translational inhibition by sugars. 3. KIN10-ox seedlings failed to show morning sucrose-pulse-induced phase advance, demonstrating a role in transient sugar entrainment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light versus high light; 90 mM sucrose supplementation; transient morning sucrose pulses
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome meta-analysis using published KIN10-regulated transcript datasets [17]
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression lengthens circadian period in low energy and likely perturbs broader energy signaling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KIN10 is treated here as a core signaling component upstream of bZIP63 rather than a metabolic enzyme or transcription factor.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates phase adjustment of the circadian oscillator in response to sugar availability
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: transcript peaks after bZIP63 in constant light
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: accumulates before dawn under low light/low energy; suppressed by sucrose and high light
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PRR7 is upregulated by bZIP63 in low-energy conditions and is required for normal sugar-responsive circadian period and phase adjustment.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotyping (prr7-11, cited and confirmed for entrainment phenotype); qRT-PCR; promoter ChIP by bZIP63
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRR7 promoter is bound by bZIP63 in a region containing bZIP motifs/G-box. 2. PRR7 transcript abundance is reduced in bzip63 loss-of-function/RNAi lines and increased in bZIP63 overexpressors. 3. PRR7 functions as the clock-output node through which sugar signaling adjusts circadian phase.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light, high light, sucrose supplementation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Late circadian phase/defective entrainment when impaired.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core downstream non-enzymatic target because it is directly manipulated and central to the demonstrated sugar-signaling mechanism.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: modulates circadian transcriptional response to sugars
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CHE transcripts are induced by sucrose; this induction is attenuated in KIN10-ox and somewhat in tps1-12
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHE modulates sugar effects on CCA1 amplitude and can enhance sucrose-induced phase advance when overexpressed, but is not required for the morning sugar-induced phase advance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; bimolecular fluorescence complementation; qRT-PCR after sucrose pulse; che mutants and CHE-ox assayed with CCA1:luc amplitude and phase response curves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHE physically interacts with bZIP63 in yeast and planta. 2. Sucrose induces CHE transcripts, and CHE affects CCA1 amplitude in a sugar-dependent manner. 3. CHE overexpression enhances sucrose-induced phase advance, but che loss-of-function mutants largely retain morning phase advance, indicating CHE is modulatory rather than essential.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 90 mM sucrose supplementation; sucrose pulses; low light/light-dark cycles
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in CHE-ox; CCA1:luc and CCA1(TBSm):luc reporters
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broader clock perturbation if overexpressed.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CHE is a transcription factor, the paper does not place it as the decisive regulator of the sugar metabolite relationship; it is a secondary modulator of the clock response.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tre6P synthesis branch linking sucrose status to SnRK1 signaling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tre6P tracks sucrose and transmits cellular energy status to circadian regulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms signaling metabolite required for sugar-responsive phase adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for Tre6P production inferred from use of hypomorphic tps1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced TPS1 function decreases or abolishes sucrose effects on circadian period and phase, showing Tre6P biosynthesis is required for sugar entrainment of the clock.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hypomorphic mutant analysis (tps1-11, tps1-12, tps1-13); CCA1:luc period assays with/without sucrose; sucrose-pulse phase response curves; DIN6:luc reporter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native TPS1 alleles; DIN6:luc and CCA1:luc reporters used for phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light; high light; 90 mM sucrose supplementation; transient morning sucrose pulses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome meta-analysis includes published OtsA/Tre6P-regulated transcript datasets [20]
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: tps1 alleles show altered metabolic phenotypes and longer circadian periods under sucrose, implying broader carbon-status defects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: tps1-12 is a weaker allele and retains partial sucrose responsiveness, consistent with weaker metabolite defects in prior work.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of PRR7 under low-energy conditions; sugar-responsive regulation linked to SnRK1-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sugar-responsive circadian entrainment via regulation of clock gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates PRR7 transcript accumulation in low-energy/low-light conditions; this upregulation is suppressed by sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: low energy/starvation; sucrose status; SnRK1/KIN10 signaling; Tre6P pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential regulator linking sugar/energy signals to circadian phase adjustment through PRR7
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: aligns circadian oscillator phase with daily energy availability
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: bZIP63 transcripts peak before PRR7 in constant light, around ZT6
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functions prominently before dawn under low light/low energy; little effect on PRR7 under high light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of bZIP63 blocks sucrose-induced shortening of circadian period and abolishes morning sucrose-pulse phase advance; overexpression increases PRR7 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants; RNAi knockdown; overexpression; ChIP-qPCR showing promoter binding; qRT-PCR for PRR7; CCA1:luc circadian assays; phase response curves
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PRR7 promoter; CHE; KIN10 (functional upstream regulator)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP63 binds the PRR7 promoter region containing bZIP motifs/G-box. 2. bZIP63 positively regulates PRR7 transcript abundance specifically in low-energy conditions. 3. bzip63 mutants lose sugar responsiveness of circadian period and phase, demonstrating bZIP63 is required for sugar entrainment of the Arabidopsis clock.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bzip63 mutants show defective entrainment and late circadian phase; constitutive misexpression may perturb broader starvation/sugar signaling networks.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low light versus high light; 90 mM sucrose supplementation; transient sucrose pulses; light/dark cycles
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in HA-bZIP63 overexpression and RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: circadian expression data referenced from GEO GSE19271
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central core gene of the paper; identified as the main sugar-responsive transcription factor connecting metabolism to the circadian oscillator.
